text: Which locally reproducible secret scanner supplies the primary M0 scan?
why_it_matters: The Charter requires secret exclusion but does not select tooling; the answer fixes local/CI parity, rules, dependency admission, and evidence format.
unlocks: SEC-FR-002, SEC-NFR-001, the secret fixture corpus, and release security evidence.

options.label: Pinned maintained scanner
options.pros: Broad ecosystem patterns and reproducible local/CI execution.

options.label: Repository-native scanner
options.pros: Full project control and no external scanner dependency.
options.cons: High maintenance burden and weaker corpus coverage.

default: Select a maintained scanner during implementation, pin and admit it through Nix/Bazel, version its rules, and retain narrow repository-specific supplemental rules.

answer.text: Option 1: use Gitleaks v8.30.1 as the primary M0 scanner, pinned by version and artifact digest through Nix/Bazel; version its offline rules and retain narrow repository-specific supplemental rules. Revisit the scanner through dependency admission if upstream security maintenance ends.
